semanticCore: Humiliated, submissive, small, low, despised, or dwarf
quranicRelevance: Entering Hell submissively (Dakhireen - 'sayadkhuloona jahannama dakhireen', 'wa kullun atahu dakhireen').

keyDerivations.meaning: Submissive, humbled, humiliated, low, or dwarfed

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلَىٰ مَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ مِنْ شَيْءٍ يَتَفَيَّأُ ظِلَالُهُ عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَالشَّمَائِلِ سُجَّدًا لِلَّهِ وَهُمْ دَاخِرُونَ (16:48): Have they not considered what things God has created? Their shadows incline to the right and left, prostrating to God while they are submissive/humbled.
discardedNoise: Savings accounts (Iddikhar) - written with thal (ذخر), distinct spelling/meaning.
summaryEn: The root represents submissive humility (Dukhoor), describing the involuntary physical submission of shadows to God and the forced humiliation of sinners in Hell.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الخضوع الذليل والصغار (داخر)، مصوراً السجود الكوني الإجباري لظلال المخلوقات لله سجوداً وخضوعاً، ودخول العصاة صاغرين لجهنم.
rawText: ادخرهُ 4 He (God, A, or a man, Msb) rendered him abject, mean, contemptible, or despicable. (S, A, Msb, K.) ادّخر 8 : see art. ذخر. دَاخِرٌ , applied to a man, Abject, mean, contemptible, or despicable. (S, TA.) دَخَرَ 1 , (S, A, Msb, K,) aor. ﹷ; (Msb, K;) and دَخِرَ, aor. ﹷ; (K;) inf. n. of the former, دُخُورٌ, (S, A, Msb, K,) and (of the latter, TA) دَخَرٌ; (K, TA; in the CK دُخْرٌ;) He was, or became, object, mean, contemptible, or despicable. (S, A, Msb, K.) مُدَّخِرٌ : see art. ذخر.
